---
title: "Getting Started with phynotype"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Getting Started with phynotype}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
library(phynotype)
```

`phynotype` provides a direct clustering workflow centered on `cluster()`, `validate()`, `explore()`, `predict()`, and explicit plotting helpers.

## Single clustering

```{r}
fit <- cluster(iris[, 1:4], method = "kmeans", k = 3, seed = 1)
fit
summary(fit)
```

## Validation

```{r}
val <- validate(fit)
val$metrics_table
```

## Exploration

```{r}
exp <- explore(fit)
head(exp$feature_summary)
head(exp$separation_table)
```

## Plotting

```{r}
plot_clusters(fit)
plot_silhouette(fit)
plot_validation(val)
plot_cluster_sizes(fit)
plot_feature_profiles(exp)
```

## Prediction

```{r}
pred <- predict(fit, iris[1:10, 1:4])
pred
```
